content:
- Finished story-11 task `02-task-e2e-receiver-crash-and-restart-recovery`: added two ignored long-lane restart-recovery scenarios, extracted typed runner lifecycle and destination-lock support, and added typed tracking-progress polling helpers.
- Full required validation passed cleanly: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long`; the long-lane rerun also verified the new restart tests alongside the existing ignored suite.
- Marked the task done with `<passes>true</passes>`, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed as `de3e97c`, and pushed `master`; the only remaining worktree change is an unrelated pre-existing deletion of `.agents/skills/info-about-repo/SKILL.md` that I intentionally left untouched.

- Read previous progress, Ralph choose-task rules, current active/done task summaries, and the active story-11 task files.
- Confirmed there are no active failing bug tasks, no visible blocked tasks, and no active priority tags changing ordering.
- Determined the actual repo is a Rust workspace (`runner`, `ingest-contract`, `source-bootstrap`) with existing long-lane e2e chaos harness support, so the most logical next task is to continue story-11 with the externally imposed network-fault test before later verification/report/reporting/CI stories.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and confirmed the active failing set is still stories 11-15, with no active failing bugs and no blocker tags on the visible candidates.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/03-task-e2e-network-fault-injection-imposed-externally.md` as the next task because the repo is currently centered on Rust long-lane end-to-end chaos validation and the existing external chaos gateway plus harness support make this the most coherent next step.
- Wrote that path to `.ralph/current_task.txt` and stopped immediately per choose-task instructions.

- Read previous Ralph progress and confirmed the active task path is `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/03-task-e2e-network-fault-injection-imposed-externally.md`.
- Loaded the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions so the next step follows the mandated planning/execution workflow.
- Next: inspect the task file contents and decide whether to create a planning document with `TO BE VERIFIED` or execute an existing approved plan.

- Read the active task file and confirmed it is still plain task metadata with no approved execution plan or `NOW EXECUTE` marker.
- Determined this turn must produce a separate plan file under the story task plan directory, leave it in verification state, and stop without implementation.
- Next: inspect prior story-11 plan files and the existing long-lane chaos harness to draft a concrete TDD-oriented execution plan.

- Confirmed the active task is in planning mode and reviewed the story-11 plan format from the two preceding tasks.
- Inspected the current long-lane harness and saw that external HTTP chaos already exists through a dedicated gateway, while the generic harness still owns runner and sink wiring details.
- Next: read the design/test-strategy references and search for any existing network-fault support so the new plan targets the right failure mode and boundary cleanup.

- Created the story-11 task-03 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/03-task-e2e-network-fault-injection-imposed-externally_plans/2026-04-19-external-network-fault-e2e-plan.md`.
- Linked that plan from `.ralph/tasks/story-11-e2e-chaos/03-task-e2e-network-fault-injection-imposed-externally.md` and ended the plan with `NOW EXECUTE` so the next turn can start straight in the TDD red-green loop.
- The plan chooses a deterministic external transport fault boundary: a typed gateway policy that aborts one matching HTTPS request before forwarding, then proves retry/resume, convergence, and a later post-recovery update.
- Next: read the task and plan, treat `NOW EXECUTE` as authoritative, then implement slice 1 by adding the ignored failing long-lane test before changing gateway or harness code.

- Read previous Ralph progress and confirmed the active task is story-11 task 03 for externally imposed network fault injection.
- Re-read the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions; this turn is execution because the prior plan already ended at NOW EXECUTE.
- Next: read the task file and execution plan, inspect the worktree and harness files, then start with the first RED long-lane test slice.

- Added the first RED tracer-bullet long-lane test for one externally imposed transport disconnect and destination convergence.
- The new scenario deliberately calls a not-yet-implemented public helper for a customer-specific external transport fault so the next compile run exposes the minimal missing interface.
- Next: run the targeted ignored test to capture the red failure, then implement the typed gateway and harness support needed for slice 1.

- Replaced the HTTP-500-only gateway boundary with a typed external sink fault model and threaded it through the generic harness/customer harness.
- Added support scaffolding for a one-shot transport disconnect before forwarding while keeping the existing HTTP-500 path on the same typed API.
- Next: compile the targeted long-lane test again, fix any integration/build errors, then run the scenario and tighten assertions for gateway-observed retry/resume.

- The external network-fault long-lane scenario is now green end to end: one injected transport disconnect, gateway-observed retry success, destination/helper convergence, post-recovery live update, and verify output.
- The gateway boundary was flattened into typed external sink faults shared by both the existing HTTP-500 test and the new transport-disconnect test.
- Next: run repository validation in order (`make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`), fix the first failing lane at a time, then update task metadata and finalize if everything is green.

- Repository validation is fully green: `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, and `make test-long` all passed after the typed gateway/context cleanup.
- The new long-lane network fault scenario passes alongside the existing HTTP-500, restart-recovery, and image long lanes.
- Next: mark the task file passed, run task switch, inspect the final diff/status one last time, then commit and push the completed task.
